completed_tasks.name: Task 0.1: Initialize electron-vite project with React + TypeScript

remaining_tasks.name: Task 0.3: Setup Tailwind CSS v4 with @theme directive
Task 1.1: Implement CSS variable theme system
Task 4.1: Wire all components, IPC handlers, and stores

blockers.description: Electron runtime not functioning correctly - require('electron') returns a string (path) instead of the electron module when running via electron-vite dev or direct electron binary
blockers.workaround: npm run dev builds successfully but cannot launch Electron app. The electron binary itself seems to be wrapping Node.js instead of running Electron.

blockers.description: better-sqlite3 12.10.0 incompatible with Electron 42's V8 API - had to downgrade to Electron 41
blockers.workaround: Electron 41.0.0 installed, better-sqlite3 compiled successfully for arm64

blockers.description: electron-store v11 is ESM-only and incompatible with Electron main process CommonJS - downgraded to v8.2.0

decisions.decision: Downgrade vite from 8.x to 7.x because electron-vite@5.0.0 requires vite@^5.0.0 || ^6.0.0 || ^7.0.0
Downgrade electron from 42 to 41 because better-sqlite3 12.10.0 is incompatible with Electron 42's V8 API
decisions.rationale: V8 API changes in Node.js 24/Electron 42 broke native module compilation

decisions.decision: Downgrade electron-store from v11 to v8.2.0 because v11 is ESM-only
decisions.rationale: electron-store v11 uses ESM exports only, incompatible with Electron main process CJS context

decisions.decision: vite version changed from ^8.0.13 to ^7.0.0 in package.json

next_action: Debug why Electron is not functioning correctly - require('electron') returns a string instead of the electron module. The electron binary appears to be wrapping Node.js instead of running the actual Electron runtime.
context_notes: Task 0.1 completed successfully (electron-vite scaffold created, committed). Task 0.2 dependencies installed after resolving multiple version conflicts. npm run dev builds successfully but fails at runtime when trying to use electron module. The core issue is that require('electron') in the built output returns a string (the electron executable path) instead of the electron module object. This prevents app.whenReady(), electron.app.getPath(), etc. from working.
